ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus / Diamond Sleet DLL search-order hijack via trusted Microsoft-signed binary in user-writable path
ucs.description: Hunts Lazarus Group's signature 'sideload from a writable folder' tradecraft — dropping a legitimate Microsoft / OEM-signed host binary (wsmprovhost.exe Comebacker loader, credwiz.exe DUSER sideload, iviewers.exe Manuscrypt loader, colorcpl.exe SmoothShifter, GoogleUpdate.exe / goopdate.dll Diamond Sleet pair, vmtoolsd.exe, snmptrap.exe, GUP.exe) into %APPDATA%, %TEMP%, %PUBLIC% or %PROGRAMDATA% alongside a malicious DLL. The Microsoft-signed loader gets hash-trust and AppLocker bypass; the DLL holds the real payload. This pair is the recurring backbone of Operation Dream Job, AppleJeus, the J
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_hash) as host_binary_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("wsmprovhost.exe","credwiz.exe","iviewers.exe","colorcpl.exe","DTAUDIT.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","GUP.exe","snmptrap.exe","RTKauUService64.exe") AND (Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Windows\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\*\\Downloads\\*") AND Processes.parent_process_name!="msiexec.exe" AND Processes.parent_process_name!="setup.exe" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=process_path "(?<host_dir>.*)\\\\[^\\\\]+$" | join type=inner dest host_dir [ | tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_hash) as dll_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rex field=file_path "(?<host_dir>.*)\\\\[^\\\\]+$" ] | where host_binary_hash!=dll_hash | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name process_path file_name file_path host_binary_hash dll_hash parent cmdline
ucs.kql:
let LazarusSideloadHosts = dynamic(["wsmprovhost.exe","credwiz.exe","iviewers.exe","colorcpl.exe","DTAUDIT.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","GUP.exe","snmptrap.exe","RTKauUService64.exe"]);
let WritableStaging = dynamic([@"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Downloads\"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (LazarusSideloadHosts)
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (WritableStaging)
// adjacent DLL — same folder as the trusted host binary
| where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256
// strip well-known side-by-side runtime libs that are commonly bundled with installers
| where FileName !in~ ("dbghelp.dll","msvcr100.dll","msvcp100.dll","vcruntime140.dll","vcruntime140_1.dll","msvcp140.dll","ucrtbase.dll")
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | project DeviceId, ProcessId = InitiatingProcessId, ProcessCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine, ParentBinary = InitiatingProcessParentFileName
  ) on $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.ProcessId, DeviceId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          HostBinary = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          HostPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          HostSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          SideloadedDll = FileName,
          SideloadedDllSHA256 = SHA256,
          ParentBinary, ProcessCmd
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LazarusSideloadHosts = dynamic(["wsmprovhost.exe","credwiz.exe","iviewers.exe","colorcpl.exe","DTAUDIT.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","GUP.exe","snmptrap.exe","RTKauUService64.exe"]);
let WritableStaging = dynamic([@"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Downloads\"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend BinaryName = tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| where tolower(BinaryName) in (LazarusSideloadHosts)
| where NewProcessName has_any (WritableStaging)
| where ParentProcessName !endswith "\\msiexec.exe"
   and ParentProcessName !endswith "\\setup.exe"
   and ParentProcessName !endswith "\\TrustedInstaller.exe"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, BinaryName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Lazarus DLL Search-Order Hijack via Trusted Microsoft-Signed Binary in User-Writable Path
id: 7ad3d2e0-6a9f-4f1b-9e1d-laz001sideload
status: experimental
description: Detects DPRK Lazarus Group / Diamond Sleet DLL sideloading by spotting known-abused signed host binaries (wsmprovhost.exe, credwiz.exe, iviewers.exe, colorcpl.exe, GoogleUpdate.exe, vmtoolsd.exe, GUP.exe, snmptrap.exe) executing from user-writable directories instead of their canonical install paths. Used in Operation Dream Job, AppleJeus, the JumpCloud intrusion, and the TeamCity CVE-2023-42793 chain.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0032/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/10/18/multiple-north-korean-threat-actors-exploiting-the-teamcity-cve-2023-42793-vulnerability/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/lazarus-attacks-jumpcloud
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/10
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\wsmprovhost.exe'
      - '\credwiz.exe'
      - '\iviewers.exe'
      - '\colorcpl.exe'
      - '\DTAUDIT.exe'
      - '\vmtoolsd.exe'
      - '\GoogleUpdate.exe'
      - '\GUP.exe'
      - '\snmptrap.exe'
      - '\RTKauUService64.exe'
  selection_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
      - '\Downloads\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
  filter_installer:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\setup.exe'
      - '\TrustedInstaller.exe'
  condition: selection_image and selection_path and not filter_installer
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate developer staging or installer self-extraction prior to copy-to-Program-Files
  - Portable software intentionally run from user folders (rare for these specific binaries)
level: high
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.001
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0032
ucs.rationale: Lazarus / Diamond Sleet has used this exact 'trusted-loader-in-writable-path + adjacent malicious DLL' construct continuously since 2020 — Comebacker (wsmprovhost), DUSER (credwiz), Manuscrypt (iviewers), SmoothShifter (colorcpl), and the 2023 TeamCity / JumpCloud intrusions. The detection deliberately keys on the unusual launch location of legitimate signed binaries — not on the DLL itself, since
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Lazarus FudModule BYOVD — vulnerable signed kernel driver service-install for admin-to-kernel EDR kill
ucs.description: Hunts Lazarus's FudModule rootkit installation pattern: registering a Windows service whose ImagePath points at a known-vulnerable signed kernel driver (Dell dbutil_2_3.sys, ENE ene.sys, MSI Afterburner RTCore64.sys, OpenLibSys WinRing0x64.sys, Intel iqvw64e.sys, Gigabyte gdrv.sys, ASRock AsrDrv101.sys, AppID downgrade) — used by Lazarus / Diamond Sleet across 2022-2024 to defeat Defender / Kaspersky / AhnLab kernel callbacks via the FudModule rootkit (CVE-2024-21338 AppLocker driver IOCTL exploitation, plus the older BYOVD chain).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Registry.process_name) as installer values(Registry.user) as user values(Registry.registry_value_data) as image_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path="*\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\*\\ImagePath" AND (Registry.registry_value_data="*dbutil_2_3.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*DBUtil_2_3.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*ene.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*RTCore64.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*WinRing0x64.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*iqvw64e.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*gdrv.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*MsIo64.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*AsrDrv101.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*procexp152.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*viragt64.sys*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*amsdk.sys*") by Registry.dest Registry.registry_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | table firstTime lastTime dest user installer registry_path image_path
ucs.kql:
let FudModuleBYOVD = dynamic(["dbutil_2_3.sys","ene.sys","rtcore64.sys","winring0x64.sys","iqvw64e.sys","gdrv.sys","msio64.sys","asrdrv101.sys","procexp152.sys","viragt64.sys","amsdk.sys","appid.sys"]);
let RegistryInstall =
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey startswith @"HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\"
    | where RegistryValueName =~ "ImagePath"
    | extend DriverFile = tolower(tostring(split(RegistryValueData, "\\")[-1]))
    | where DriverFile in (FudModuleBYOVD)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("TrustedInstaller.exe","msiexec.exe")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, ActionType, RegistryKey, DriverImagePath = RegistryValueData,
              DriverFile, Installer = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              InstallerCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              InstallerUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessSHA256;
let DriverDrop =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | extend DriverFile = tolower(FileName)
    | where DriverFile in (FudModuleBYOVD)
    | where FolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\System32\drivers\"
         or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\Users\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\AppData\", @"\Public\", @"\Temp\")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, ActionType, FolderPath, FileName, DriverFile, SHA256,
              Dropper = InitiatingProcessFileName, DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              DropperUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName;
union RegistryInstall, DriverDrop
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let FudModuleBYOVD = dynamic(["dbutil_2_3.sys","ene.sys","rtcore64.sys","winring0x64.sys","iqvw64e.sys","gdrv.sys","msio64.sys","asrdrv101.sys","procexp152.sys","viragt64.sys","amsdk.sys","appid.sys"]);
// Service install via Registry (System / Sysmon EID 13)
let RegInstall = WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 13
    | extend TargetObject = tostring(EventData.TargetObject),
             Details = tostring(EventData.Details),
             Image = tostring(EventData.Image)
    | where TargetObject startswith @"HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\"
         and TargetObject endswith @"\ImagePath"
    | extend DriverFile = tolower(tostring(split(Details, "\\")[-1]))
    | where DriverFile in (FudModuleBYOVD)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Source = "Sysmon Registry", DriverFile, DriverImagePath = Details, Installer = Image, RegistryKey = TargetObject;
// Backup via classic Windows service-install event (System log EID 7045)
let ServiceInstall = Event
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventLog == "System" and EventID == 7045
    | extend ImagePath = tostring(extract(@"<Data Name=['\"]ImagePath['\"]>([^<]+)", 1, EventData)),
             ServiceName = tostring(extract(@"<Data Name=['\"]ServiceName['\"]>([^<]+)", 1, EventData))
    | extend DriverFile = tolower(tostring(split(ImagePath, "\\")[-1]))
    | where DriverFile in (FudModuleBYOVD)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Source = "System 7045", DriverFile, DriverImagePath = ImagePath, Installer = "", RegistryKey = ServiceName;
union RegInstall, ServiceInstall
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Lazarus FudModule BYOVD - Vulnerable Kernel Driver Service Install
id: 8b14e7a2-2c3f-44a6-8b1c-laz002fudmod
status: experimental
description: Detects DPRK Lazarus Group / Diamond Sleet installing a Windows kernel-driver service whose ImagePath points at a known-vulnerable signed driver used by the FudModule rootkit (Dell dbutil_2_3.sys, ENE ene.sys, MSI RTCore64.sys, OpenLibSys WinRing0x64.sys, Intel iqvw64e.sys, Gigabyte gdrv.sys, ASRock AsrDrv101.sys, etc.) for admin-to-kernel privilege escalation and EDR kernel-callback removal.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0032/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2024/02/28/lazarus-fudmodule-rootkit-cve-2024-21338/
  - https://decoded.avast.io/janvojtesek/lazarus-and-the-fudmodule-rootkit-beyond-byovd-with-an-admin-to-kernel-zero-day/
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2022/09/30/amazon-themed-campaigns-lazarus-netherlands-belgium/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/10
logsource:
  category: registry_set
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_key:
    TargetObject|startswith: 'HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\'
    TargetObject|endswith: '\ImagePath'
  selection_driver:
    Details|contains:
      - 'dbutil_2_3.sys'
      - 'ene.sys'
      - 'RTCore64.sys'
      - 'WinRing0x64.sys'
      - 'iqvw64e.sys'
      - 'gdrv.sys'
      - 'MsIo64.sys'
      - 'AsrDrv101.sys'
      - 'procexp152.sys'
      - 'viragt64.sys'
      - 'amsdk.sys'
  filter_legit_installer:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\TrustedInstaller.exe'
      - '\msiexec.exe'
  condition: selection_key and selection_driver and not filter_legit_installer
falsepositives:
  - Hardware-vendor utility (Dell Command Update, MSI Afterburner, Gigabyte Control Center) install via vendor MSI — verify Image is a signed vendor installer
  - Legitimate IT troubleshooting (Process Explorer driver) on engineer endpoints
level: critical
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.privilege_escalation
  - attack.t1543.003
  - attack.t1068
  - attack.t1014
  - attack.t1562.001
  - attack.g0032
ucs.rationale: Microsoft (Feb 2024) and Avast (Dec 2023) attribute the FudModule rootkit exclusively to Lazarus / Diamond Sleet, with this exact BYOVD chain documented across the 3CX intrusion (Dell DBUtil), the Aug 2023 aerospace campaigns (ene.sys), and the Feb 2024 CVE-2024-21338 admin-to-kernel exploitation (vulnerable AppID.sys downgrade). Lazarus is one of the few state actors whose tradecraft REQUIRES ker

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `System` is not a canonical column of Event, WindowsEvent.
`ServiceName` is not a column of Event, WindowsEvent; it does exist on SecurityEvent.

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
